input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
There are a few things beyond `SafeAreaEdges="None"` that commonly cause this:

**1. Hide the navigation bar** if you're inside a `Shell` or `NavigationPage`:

```xml
<!-- If using Shell -->
<ContentPage Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None">

<!-- If using NavigationPage -->
<ContentPage NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None">
```

**2. Make the image fill its container:**

```xml
<Image Source="photo.jpg"
       Aspect="AspectFill"
       HorizontalOptions="Fill"
       VerticalOptions="Fill" />
```

**3. On Android, enable edge-to-edge drawing.** Starting with Android 15, apps must opt in to drawing behind system bars. In your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);

    if (OperatingSystem.IsAndroidVersionAtLeast(35))
    {
        // Android 15+ enforces edge-to-edge, but for older versions:
        Window?.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
        Window?.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
    }

    // Draw behind system bars
    AndroidX.Core.View.WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window!, false);
}
```

**4. On iOS, check for extra layout padding.** Ensure no parent layout (e.g., `Grid`, `VerticalStackLayout`) has `Padding` set.

**Minimal full example:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None">
    <Grid>
        <Image Source="photo.jpg"
               Aspect="AspectFill"
               HorizontalOptions="Fill"
               VerticalOptions="Fill" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

The most common culprit is **#3** — `SafeAreaEdges="None"` handles the MAUI layout layer, but Android still clips content to the area between system bars unless you call `SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(window, false)`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is working on a .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10 and wants an image to go edge-to-edge (behind status bar and navigation bar), but setting `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage isn't working. Let me help them diagnose this.

In .NET MAUI, achieving edge-to-edge content involves a few things:

1. **Safe area insets** — `SafeAreaEdges` controls which edges respect safe area insets. Setting it to `None` should allow content to go behind the status bar/navigation bar areas.

2. **However**, there's more to it in .NET MAUI / .NET 10. 

On Android, edge-to-edge rendering is now the default behavior in recent versions, but window insets still need proper handling. On iOS, `SafeAreaEdges` should theoretically work, but the issue often comes from the NavigationPage or Shell adding its own padding, or the page itself having margins that prevent the content from truly reaching the edges. I should also check if the view itself has `IgnoreSafeArea` set properly, since individual controls can override the page-level setting. For .NET MAUI in .NET 9+, `SafeAreaEdges="None"` should theoretically let content extend behind system UI, but the real culprits are usually the navigation bar still being visible, Android needing transparent system bars configured, or the image not using `AspectFill`/`Fill` to actually fill the space. The most practical fixes are disabling the navigation bar with `Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"`, handling platform-specific transparency on Android, and checking that parent layouts aren't adding unwanted padding. I should also consider that on Android, edge-to-edge rendering might require additional setup like making the status and navigation bars transparent, and in .NET 10 MAUI this is becoming more of a default behavior following Android 15's requirements. Let me put together a comprehensive answer addressing these common issues.
